flashcards.q: In which year was the last Mauryan emperor Brihadratha assassinated?
Who assassinated the last Mauryan emperor to found the Śhunga dynasty?
Name the Vedic ritual revival by Puṣhyamitra Śhunga to establish his power.
Which scholar compiled the 'Yoga Sūtras' during the Śhunga period?
Identify the geographical region ruled by the Sātavāhanas.
flashcards.a: The Deccan (modern Maharashtra, Telangana, and Andhra Pradesh).

flashcards.q: Which powerful Sātavāhana queen had a major inscription carved at Nāśhik?
In which caves near Pune did the Sātavāhanas collect tolls and taxes?
What is the name of the Sātavāhana capital known today as Paithan?
Name the Chedi king of Kalinga who documented his victories in the Hāthīgumphā inscription.
Where were the rock-cut caves for Jain monks built by the Chedis?
Which South Indian dam is called the Grand Anicut?
Name the ancient port city of the Cholas identified with Kāveripattinam.
Which South Indian kingdom had its capital at Madurai and was known for pearls?
Identify the capital of the Chera kingdom (Keralaputra).
Name the famous Tamil epic telling the story of Kaṇṇagi.
What group of people entered through the northwest and became known as 'Indo-Greeks'?
flashcards.a: Alexander's satraps who established independent domains.

flashcards.q: True or False: The Heliodorus pillar is dedicated to the deity Shiva.
flashcards.a: False. It is dedicated to Vāsudeva (Vishnu/Krishna).

flashcards.q: Which era/calendar starts 78 years after the Gregorian year 0?
Name the most powerful Kuṣhāṇa ruler.
Which major trade route did the Kuṣhāṇas control?
Which art school used grey-black schist stone and showed realistic anatomy?
Which art school used red sandstone and is known for its indigenous Indian style?
Who is the god of wealth frequently depicted in Mathurā art with a moustache?
Identify the script used in many inscriptions of this period, including the Sātavāhanas and Chedis.
Which two major Indian epics were reaching their final composed form during this era?
